svg.aria-label: SM_120 FP32 (FFMA) 4-Stage Pipeline Model with ILP Utilization Visualization
svg.text: Measured pipeline depth = 4 stages; textbook ILP scaling confirms clean in-order design with no structural hazards
Pipeline Occupancy at Different ILP Levels (time →)
Saturation at ILP=4 → No benefit beyond 4 independent chains
Why 92% and not 100%? — The 7% Dispatch Tax

g.text: 128 FFMA ops completed in 139 cycles (measured at ILP=4)
• ~9 cyc: loop control instructions (BRA, ISETP) consuming issue slots

svg.text: loss is in instruction mix, not pipe capacity

g.text: • ILP=2 → 2.00× (exact): Perfect doubling proves zero structural hazards — clean in-order pipeline with no write-back port conflicts.
• Shallow depth (4 vs 8+ on Ampere/Hopper): SM_120 trades depth for lower latency; only 4 independent chains needed for saturation.
• Practical implication: Kernel register pressure is modest — 4 accumulators (not 8+) suffice for peak FP32 throughput per warp.
• IADD3 is even shallower: Only ILP=2 needed (2-stage pipe) — address computation saturates with minimal register pressure.
